differential_diagnoses: Pyoderma gangrenosum (post-surgical pathergy and systemic involvement)
Systemic neutrophilic disease (PASH-associated or systemic Sweet's syndrome)
Crohn's disease with systemic extraintestinal manifestations
Neutrophilic panniculitis with internal organ involvement
next_steps: Perform excisional biopsy of the margin of an active skin ulcer for histopathology and tissue culture to confirm neutrophilic dermatosis and exclude infection
Initiate high-dose systemic glucocorticoids (e.g., intravenous methylprednisolone) as the primary treatment for presumed neutrophilic dermatosis
Urgent multidisciplinary consultation with Rheumatology and Dermatology
Perform colonoscopy or specialized imaging (e.g., CTE/MRE) to evaluate for underlying undiagnosed inflammatory bowel disease
Monitor inflammatory markers (CRP, ESR, ferritin) as a proxy for clinical response to immunosuppressive therapy
Discontinue broad-spectrum antibiotics once infection is definitively ruled out by biopsy and clinical stabilization occurs
